ocr.text: In the Asia-Pacific region, the mining and metals industry is increasingly under scrutiny regarding its environmental impact and
sustainability practices, particularly in relation to tailings management. As a critical component of mining operations, effective
tailings management is essential not only for minimizing environmental risks but also for ensuring compliance with stringent
regulatory frameworks that govern mining activities. The financial implications of these management practices are significant,
with companies facing rising costs associated with Operating Costs and Maintenance Costs, among others.
CostType  Amount  CostRatios
DirectCosts  IndirectCosts  TotalCosts  CostIndices
OperatingCosts  MaintenanceCosts  EnvironmentalCosts  ClosureCosts  RegulatoryCosts
SGD20M  SGD5M  SGD25M  0.8  1.5
SGD10M  SGD3M  SGD13M  0.6  1.2
SGD8M  SGD2M  SGD10M  0.5  1.0
SGD30M  SGD10M  SGD40M  1.0  2.0
SGD15M  SGD4M  SGD19M  0.7  1.3
Freeport-McMoRan, a key player in the mining sector, has acknowledged the necessity of investing in advanced tailings
management systems to mitigate potential environmental hazards and meet regulatory requirements. The company's
commitment to sustainability is reflected in its approach to managing tailings, which entails comprehensive planning and
execution strategies to minimize the environmental footprint of mining operations. A considerable portion of Freeport-
McMoRan's budget is allocated to Environmental Costs, which encompass investments in technology and infrastructure aimed

at reducing the ecological impact associated with tailings storage and disposal. 

Furthermore, the growing demand from
stakeholders for transparency and accountability in environmental practices has prompted Freeport-McMoRan to enhance its
reporting on tailings management costs. This data not only serves to document the financial investments made in tailings
management but also aids in evaluating the effectiveness of these investments in achieving long-term sustainability goals. The
associated costs of Closure and Regulatory compliance are critical components of this financial assessment, as they reflect the

company's proactive stance in planning for the lifecycle of mining operations and the subsequent reclamation of sites.

The
accompanying table outlines the detailed breakdown of Tailings Management Costs incurred by Freeport-McMoRan across
various operational categories. By providing insights into specific metrics such as Operating Costs and Environmental Costs,
the data enables stakeholders to gauge the financial commitments required to uphold industry standards while promoting
sustainable practices. As the Asia-Pacific region continues to evolve in terms of regulatory expectations and environmental
stewardship, the analysis of these costs will play a vital role in shaping the future strategies of mining companies, ensuring they
not only meet current demands but also set benchmarks for responsible mining in the years to come.
